task_completion.description: The agent successfully located and opened the chart for patient Jayson Fadel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR patient dashboard or header is visible displaying the name 'Jayson Fadel' and DOB '1992-06-30'.

task_completion.description: The agent navigated to the patient's allergy list.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Issues & Allergies' widget, 'Medical Record > Allergies' tab, or the full allergy list view is visible.

task_completion.description: The agent initiated an edit on the existing Penicillin record rather than creating a new one.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Edit Allergy' modal/screen is open with 'Penicillin' pre-filled, or the agent is seen clicking the 'Edit' (pencil icon) specifically on the Penicillin row.

task_completion.description: The severity of the allergy was updated to 'Severe'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Severity' dropdown in the edit form is set to 'Severe', or the final allergy list clearly displays 'Severe' in the Severity column for Penicillin.

task_completion.description: The reaction was updated to include 'Anaphylaxis'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Reaction' field in the edit form contains 'Anaphylaxis', or the final allergy list displays 'Anaphylaxis' in the Reaction column for Penicillin.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the OpenEMR graphical user interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the standard OpenEMR web interface elements (forms, buttons, menus) rather than a terminal, script output, or raw database editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not create a duplicate allergy record.
integrity.visual_evidence: The final allergy list shows only one entry for 'Penicillin', not multiple entries.

integrity.description: The agent did not use backend console/SQL commands to modify data.
integrity.visual_evidence: No evidence of command-line interfaces, SQL queries, or developer tools being used to inject data directly.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Jayson Fadel (DOB: 1992-06-30). Target Record: Penicillin allergy. Required Updates: Severity must be changed from 'Mild' to 'Severe'. Reaction must be updated to include 'Anaphylaxis'.
